sanskrit:
योगिनामपि सर्वेषां मद्गतेनान्तरात्मना।
श्रद्धावान्भजते यो मां स मे युक्ततमो मतः॥
transliteration:
yoginām api sarveṣāṃ mad-gatenāntar-ātmanā |
śraddhāvān bhajate yo māṃ sa me yuktatamo mataḥ ||
literal_meaning: Of all yogīs, the one who with mind fixed on Me within, full of faith, lovingly serves Me—he is deemed by Me the most united in yoga.
contextual_meaning: Krishna crowns the path of love-and-service (bhakti) as the apex of every psychological and spiritual discipline; inner absorption plus relational devotion outperforms any solo technique.
psychological_state: Secure attachment to the transcendent: the mind has a safe base (Krishna) so it can relax its hyper-vigilance and merge loving action with being.
trigger_scenario: After months of breath-work, cold-plunge routines and productivity hacks you still feel hollow; one evening you spontaneously sing along to a kirtan playlist and cry—something in you finally feels ‘at home’.
anchor_text: Love God with steadfast heart—this is the highest yoga.
